description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped dark stone with a roughly triangular profile and a matte, slightly rough surface. The stone is predominantly black with scattered lighter speckles and small patches, giving it a natural, unpolished appearance. One face of the stone features a visible white marking or inscription, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The edges are uneven and jagged, with some areas appearing more angular while others are more rounded. The stone's thickness is relatively modest compared to its length and width, making it appear somewhat flat when viewed from the side. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, such as a park or landscaped area.
description: A dark, irregularly shaped stone with a matte, speckled surface and visible cataloging marks. Its uneven edges and flat profile make it a natural-looking urban barrier.
description_view_0: The broadest face of the stone is visible, showing a dark surface with scattered light speckles and a white cataloging mark.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the stone's thin, flat profile and jagged edges.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, with a similar dark, speckled texture but no visible markings.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, highlighting the stone's thinness and irregular edge contours.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular speckling with small white patches and a hand-applied cataloging mark; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears naturally rough and unpolished, with minor signs of abrasion but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Would visually contrast with smooth, man-made surfaces but blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; not covered by vegetation.
